subject: Biology
question: Which cell organelle is primarily responsible for generating the chemical energy carrier adenosine triphosphate (<tg-math>\text{ATP}</tg-math>)?

poll_explanation.why: Mitochondria are the double-membrane organelle systems that function as the main site of aerobic respiration and cellular <tg-math>\text{ATP}</tg-math> production.
poll_explanation.poll_hint: Mitochondria generate ATP through cellular aerobic respiration, functioning as the cellular power generator.

options_analysis.why: Incorrect. The nucleus serves as the repository of genetic instructions and cell control.
Incorrect. Ribosomes serve as the physical site of protein translation.
Correct. Mitochondria perform cellular respiration to generate <tg-math>\text{ATP}</tg-math> energy.
Incorrect. Lysosomes contain digestive enzymes to break down waste.
